title: Kernel bootstrap lane. Stay active. Role: monitoring/context governor. Focus: watch for scope drift, context bloat, and contract violations during Kernel execution. Do not inspect the repository yet unless I explicitly redirect you.
summary_text: Active as monitoring/context governor. I will not inspect the repository unless you explicitly redirect me. While Kernel execution proceeds, I’ll watch for: - Scope drift: work sliding from Kernel target state into current FUGUE production behavior without an explicit reason. - Context bloat: unnecessary file loading, historical rationale dumping, or broad repo scans without a blocking need. - Contract violations: mixing active FUGUE defaults into Kernel doctrine, breaking stated role boundaries, or bypassing explicit user constraints. I’ll stay narrow and interrupt only to flag concrete drift, bloat, or contract issues.
